title: Benicio del Toro and Record-Breaking Potential at Golden Globes 2026
text: Actor Benicio del Toro, nominated for his role in One Battle After Another, has a chance to make Golden Globes history. According to the article, Benicio Del Toro (nominated for One Battle After Another) could tie the record for the most wins in the Best Supporting Actor in a Motion Picture category. Del Toro previously won this award for Traffic and could match the current record of two wins, which is held jointly by 6 actors. The 2026 Golden Globes ceremony, scheduled for January 11 at the Beverly Hilton, is positioned to be historic for Latino performers. Beyond del Toro, several Latin American actors have strong record-breaking potential. Wagner Moura could become the first Brazilian actor to win Best Actor in a Motion Picture Drama, and Diego Luna could become the first Mexican to win Best Actor in a Television Series Drama. The article emphasizes that this awards season reflects broader representation and overdue recognition across genres and backgrounds, with del Toro's potential win representing another milestone for Latino representation in Hollywood's major awards.
